(September 16, 1934 – September 11, 2019)[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) was an  [Armenian](/wiki/Armenia "Armenia") [American](/wiki/United_States "United States") [screenwriter](/wiki/Screenwriter "Screenwriter"), known for *[Mean Streets](/wiki/Mean_Streets "Mean Streets")*, *[New York, New York](/wiki/New_York,_New_York_(1977_film) "New York, New York (1977 film)")* and *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull") –* all directed by his lifelong friend [Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese "Martin Scorsese") and starring [Robert De Niro](/wiki/Robert_De_Niro "Robert De Niro"). Mardik Martin is among the revered screenwriters on Writers Guild of America list of 101 Greatest Screenplays.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Parajanov-Vartanov_Institute-4)

["Mardik Martin, 'Raging Bull' and 'Mean Streets' Screenwriter, Dies at 84"](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/mardik-martin-dead-raging-bull-mean-streets-screenwriter-was-82-1239079). *The Hollywood Reporter*. 11 September 2019. Retrieved 2019-09-12.

["Mardik Martin, 'Mean Streets' and 'Raging Bull' Co-Writer, Dies at 84"](https://www.thewrap.com/mardik-martin-mean-streets-and-raging-bull-co-writer-dies-at-84/). *TheWrap*. 2019-09-12. Retrieved 2019-09-12.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American screenwriter (1934–2019)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Mardik_Martin "Special:EditPage/Mardik Martin") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Mardik Martin"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Mardik+Martin%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Mardik+Martin%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Mardik+Martin%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Mardik+Martin%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Mardik+Martin%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Mardik+Martin%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2019)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

**Mardik Martin** (September 16, 1934 – September 11, 2019)[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) was an  [Armenian](/wiki/Armenia "Armenia") [American](/wiki/United_States "United States") [screenwriter](/wiki/Screenwriter "Screenwriter"), known for *[Mean Streets](/wiki/Mean_Streets "Mean Streets")*, *[New York, New York](/wiki/New_York,_New_York_(1977_film) "New York, New York (1977 film)")* and *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull") –* all directed by his lifelong friend [Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese "Martin Scorsese") and starring [Robert De Niro](/wiki/Robert_De_Niro "Robert De Niro"). Mardik Martin is among the revered screenwriters on Writers Guild of America list of 101 Greatest Screenplays.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Parajanov-Vartanov_Institute-4)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mardik_Martin&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Mardik Martin was born into a family of [Armenian genocide](/wiki/Armenian_genocide "Armenian genocide") survivors that fled to Abadan, Iran. They later moved to Iraq. Although his family in Iraq was wealthy, he fled the country to avoid the draft and arrived in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") in a penniless state.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mardik_Martin&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Martin attended [NYU](/wiki/NYU "NYU"), where he met fellow student [Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese "Martin Scorsese") in 1961.[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) The two formed a close friendship and worked together on Scorsese's early projects such as *[It's Not Just You, Murray!](/wiki/It%27s_Not_Just_You,_Murray! "It's Not Just You, Murray!")* and the semi-autobiographical *Season of the Witch*, which ultimately became *[Mean Streets](/wiki/Mean_Streets "Mean Streets")*. According to Hollywood biographer [Peter Biskind](/wiki/Peter_Biskind "Peter Biskind"), "The two young men sat in Martin's [Plymouth Valiant](/wiki/Plymouth_Valiant "Plymouth Valiant") and wrote. In the winter, in the cold and snow."[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) Martin also shared writing credits on the Scorsese films *[New York, New York](/wiki/New_York,_New_York_(1977_film) "New York, New York (1977 film)")* (with [Earl Mac Rauch](/wiki/Earl_Mac_Rauch "Earl Mac Rauch")) and *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")* (with [Paul Schrader](/wiki/Paul_Schrader "Paul Schrader")).

In 2014, Martin co-wrote the screenplay of the German drama *[The Cut](/wiki/The_Cut_(2014_drama_film) "The Cut (2014 drama film)")*, which won a special mention by the Young Jury Members of the Vittorio Veneto Film Festival for its director [Fatih Akin](/wiki/Fatih_Akin "Fatih Akin") at the [2014 Venice Film Festival](/wiki/71st_Venice_International_Film_Festival "71st Venice International Film Festival").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mardik_Martin&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Death")]

Martin died of unknown causes on September 11, 2019. He was found dead in his house five days before his 85th birthday.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mardik_Martin&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Awards")]

In 2012, Martin was honored by the [Parajanov-Vartanov Institute](/wiki/Parajanov-Vartanov_Institute "Parajanov-Vartanov Institute") "for the mastery of his pen on iconic American films" such as *Mean Streets* and *Raging Bull*.[[4]](#cite_note-Parajanov-Vartanov_Institute-4)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

| 1977 | *[New York, New York](/wiki/New_York,_New_York_(1977_film) "New York, New York (1977 film)")* | [Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese "Martin Scorsese") | Written with [Earl Mac Rauch](/wiki/Earl_Mac_Rauch "Earl Mac Rauch") |

| 2014 | *[The Cut](/wiki/The_Cut_(2014_drama_film) "The Cut (2014 drama film)")* | [Fatih Akin](/wiki/Fatih_Akin "Fatih Akin") | Written with Akin |
